DateClientRoleICP Min SentimentCraft

Every call is scored on two dimensions — client sentiment and consulting craft — using recognized industry-standard rubrics (1–5).

Sentiment

Industry Standard — CSAT / Customer-Health Sentiment

The recognized SaaS approach: a CSAT-style satisfaction score (1–5), the sentiment component of a Customer Health Score. It reads the balance of positive vs. negative / dissatisfaction language the client uses, independent of consultant technique.

  • 5 — Very satisfied: overwhelmingly positive language, no friction.
  • 4 — Satisfied: mostly positive, only minor concerns.
  • 3 — Neutral: mixed or balanced positive and negative.
  • 2 — Dissatisfied: negative language outweighs positive.
  • 1 — Very dissatisfied: strongly negative or escalation language.

Benchmark: average B2B SaaS CSAT runs ~78%, leaders 85%+; best-practice health scores blend 5–7 weighted factors, of which sentiment is one.

Sources: Salesforce — CSAT · ChurnZero — Customer Health Score · Retently — CSAT benchmarks

Craft

Industry Standard — Consulting Delivery Quality

How well the consultant ran the engagement (1–5), scored on the competencies recognized across professional-services and management-consulting standards.

  • 5 — Exceptional: structured and expert throughout; strong discovery, clear next steps, real knowledge transfer — the client leaves more capable.
  • 4 — Strong: well-run and clear with solid outcomes; only minor gaps.
  • 3 — Competent: adequate delivery and some structure; limited enablement or follow-through.
  • 2 — Developing: disorganized or unclear in places; weak discovery or next steps.
  • 1 — Poor: little structure or clarity; no outcomes or knowledge transfer.

Assessed across:

  • Preparation & engagement management: agenda, structure, and control of the session.
  • Discovery & needs assessment: asks the right questions; understands the client's situation before prescribing.
  • Technical expertise & methodology: applies standards and best practices with credible domain knowledge.
  • Communication & professionalism: clear, jargon-free, courteous, responsive.
  • Outcome orientation & next steps: drives to decisions, action items, and clear expectations.
  • Knowledge transfer & enablement: builds the client's capability rather than dependence.

Sources: ICMCI/CMC Competence Framework · SFIA — Consultancy
